title: Conflict Stages, Semantic Resolution, and Rerere · Repository Checkpoint
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for conflict stages, semantic resolution, and rerere and requires the smallest safe three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, stage 3 theirs, unmerged entries, and higher-order conflict metadata before editing.
Distinguish content, add/add, delete/modify, rename, directory/file, binary, mode, submodule, and recursive conflicts and select evidence for each.
Reconstruct intent from base and both histories, produce a semantic combined result, and test invariants that neither side tested alone.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Case G38 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict. The learner must predict stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction, run git ls-files --unmerged, explain all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context, test this changed condition: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer. Competency probe: Record conflict cause, chosen invariant, rerere provenance, changed-case tests, and abort or continuation evidence for future integrators.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for record conflict cause, chosen invariant, rerere provenance, changed-case tests, and abort or continuation evidence for future integrators.

options.text: Run the Gita Conflict Regression Handoff boundary case for three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Accept the Gita Conflict Regression Handoff result because visible three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier output is the highest authority. This does not resolve diagnose the hidden failure.

For diagnose the hidden failure, apply Gita Conflict Regression Handoff without its stated preconditions, omit the three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Conflict Regression Handoff for three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Gita Conflict Regression Handoff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G45 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict. The learner must predict stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction, run git ls-files --unmerged, explain all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context, test this changed condition: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer. steps.why: Retrieving Gita Conflict Regression Handoff through the concrete three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record conflict cause, chosen invariant, rerere provenance, changed-case tests, and abort or continuation evidence for future integrators.
